storyboard.content: I keep getting this fluttering feeling in my chest, like my heart is skipping beats. It's been bothering me for a while now.
I get a bit lightheaded when I climb stairs — it never used to happen before. It's a new thing for me.
I have occasional discomfort in my chest, but it's not really pain. More like a vague uncomfortable feeling.
These symptoms have been gradually building up over the past three months. Nothing came on suddenly — it's been a slow creep.
The palpitations are especially noticeable when I'm lying down trying to sleep at night. That's when I feel them most.
The symptoms are annoying and I definitely notice them, but they're not stopping me from doing my day-to-day stuff. It's mainly bothersome at night and when I'm climbing stairs.
I'm a 46-year-old woman. I don't have any known heart problems or other major medical conditions.
